channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Barbie Imperial’s Performance as Jamilah Abubakar Highlights the Emotional Depth of “Blood vs Duty”
item.description: The article highlights Barbie Imperial’s portrayal of Jamilah Abubakar in the television series “Blood vs Duty,” recognising the role as one of her strongest and most emotionally compelling performanc...

item.title: Cross-Generational Voices Advocate for Nuclear Disarmament and Peace
item.description: The webinar 'Nuclear Bombs Never Again' will take place on August 4th at 5pm Pacific Time (8pm Eastern Time) to discuss the intersection of Japan-Korea-US policy, nuclear militarization, and anti-war ...

item.title: St. Thomas Man Faces Kidnapping and Sexual Assault Charges in London
item.description: A man from St. Thomas, Ontario, has been charged with kidnapping and sexual assault following an investigation into alleged crimes in London. The case, reported by CTV News, highlights the serious nat...

item.title: Karachi Police Kill Suspect in Six-Year-Old Girl's Murder Case After Shootout
item.description: Karachi police killed a suspect accused of abducting and murdering a six-year-old girl during a shootout after he opened fire on officers during a raid in the Keamari area. The police spokesperson con...

item.title: Pendlebury and McRae criticise Adelaide crowd after Isaac Quaynor booed throughout Collingwood's win
item.description: Collingwood veteran Scott Pendlebury has criticised sections of the Adelaide Oval crowd after defender Isaac Quaynor was repeatedly booed during the Magpies' 34-point comeback victory over Adelaide. P...

item.title: Alejandro Garnacho Loans To Villa After Poor Chelsea Spell
item.description: Alejandro Garnacho, 22, has completed a season-long loan move to Aston Villa from Chelsea after a challenging first season in England. The winger, who joined Chelsea from Manchester United in 2025, sc...

item.title: Ramaphosa's ethical breach and the ANC's role in his political crisis
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa faces a critical institutional threat as the Western Cape High Court prepares to rule on his attempt to halt parliament's Section 89 impeachment committee. The article argues...

item.title: Oman highlights Ngorongoro's natural heritage
item.description: Oman has expressed admiration for the natural beauty of Ngorongoro, a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its pristine landscapes and wildlife. The country's praise comes amid discussions about conse...

item.title: Education Department Removes Tool for Proving Racial Discrimination
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education has eliminated a decades-old regulatory tool known as disparate impact analysis, which allowed civil rights complaints against schools to use statistical disparities a...

item.title: Thompson Medical Centre recruits 21 doctors to address London's primary care shortage
item.description: Thompson Medical Centre in London, Ontario, has successfully recruited 21 new doctors since 2025, providing primary care for an estimated 40,000 patients. Director Dan Muldoon attributes this success ...

item.title: Concert Queuing Dispute: Balancing Early Arrival and Late Entry
item.description: The article explores a couple's disagreement over concert queuing etiquette. Fay insists on arriving early to secure front-row spots and watch support acts, while Odysseus prefers arriving later for t...

item.title: Danni's cancer diagnosis prompts reevaluation of childhood diet and ultra-processed foods
item.description: Danni's experience with bowel cancer has led her to radically rethink her dietary habits, particularly the role of ultra-processed foods in her health. After her diagnosis, she removed nearly all proc...

item.title: BBC lawsuit challenges Trump's executive privilege claims in January 6th case
item.description: The BBC's $10 billion defamation lawsuit against former President Donald Trump is poised to challenge his attempts to use executive privilege to block testimony in a criminal investigation related to ...

item.title: India's Ethanol Policy: Lessons from Brazil's Decades-Long Transition
item.description: The article compares India's rapid ethanol policy transition with Brazil's long-term approach, highlighting key differences in strategy and challenges. Brazil's ethanol journey began in 1931 with mand...

item.title: Anti-Immigration Sentiment and Social Media Influence in South Africa
item.description: The article explores how anti-immigrant sentiment in South Africa is being amplified through social media platforms, with tactics including AI-generated content and references to Nelson Mandela's lega...

item.title: House Approves New State Police Legislation

item.title: Tanzania's Energy Growth Highlights Renewable Potential
